title: Project Report: Microsoft Fabric Lakehouse Analytics — Power BI Analyst
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Build Fabric lakehouse: ingest raw data, transform in notebooks, semantic model, and executive dashboard.
objectives: Build semantic model in Direct Lake mode
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**Microsoft Fabric Lakehouse Analytics** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for Power BI Analyst. Duration: 5–6 weeks. Build Fabric lakehouse: ingest raw data, transform in notebooks, semantic model, and executive dashboard. Tools: Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, OneLake.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Create Fabric workspace and lakehouse
Objective: Ingest CSV/Parquet with pipelines
Objective: Build semantic model in Direct Lake mode
Objective: Executive dashboard with drill-through
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Power BI Analyst portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Power BI Analyst resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Power BI Analyst resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: Fabric workspace; Pipeline definition; Published report; Architecture diagram. Skills demonstrated: Fabric, Lakehouse, Pipelines. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Fabric workspace
Deliverable: Pipeline definition
Deliverable: Published report
Deliverable: Architecture diagram
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Fabric workspace and passes the rubric.
